ksa_text: Evaluate and develop AI workforce structure, resources, and requirements, including competency framework definition, role design, hiring process management, and team composition for technical depth and cross-functional capability balance.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of information technology (IT) acquisition/procurement requirements.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Leadership & Strategy is a primary or secondary domain for the Director of Data Governance. This task KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (director).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Leadership & Strategy), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.483). External reviewers may question whether this specific task KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the Director of Data Governance versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: McKinsey, Gartner, and Harvard Business Review consistently identify leadership and strategy skills as core differentiators for C-suite and VP data leaders.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with Leadership & Strategy appearing in job postings and role definition literature for Director of Data Governance.

decision_rationale: Leadership & Strategy task KSA is within scope for the Director of Data Governance at the director tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
